quality_notes: Add vulnerability: mention a mistake, failure, or lesson learned. Try 'I was wrong', 'took me years to learn', or 'finally understood'.
Remove unsupported numeric claims and metrics unless the topic explicitly provided them.
Remove unsupported hard stats (percentages, millions, salary figures) unless provided in the topic.
Add more relevant emojis to improve scanability and engagement (target: 4 to 10).
Align the post more closely to the planned diagram labels so the image and text tell the same story.
Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.
Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
📌 AI

I think Railway's $100 million funding to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure is a bold move that will pay off. As a Staff Engineer, I've seen how traditional cloud infrastructure can be a bottleneck for AI workloads. Railway's approach is technically interesting because it's designed from the ground up to handle AI-specific requirements.

$100 million funding
2 million developers
AI-native cloud infrastructure

With 2 million developers already on board, Railway has a significant user base to feedback into their development cycle. I believe their focus on AI-native infrastructure will give them a competitive edge over AWS. Will Railway's AI-native cloud infrastructure be the future of cloud computing? 💬 Can AWS keep up with the AI-driven demands of modern applications?

#AIcloud #CloudInfrastructure #Railway #AWS #CloudComputing #AIworkloads
